title: Iqbal Verse #12 — Allama Iqbal Urdu Shayari
content:
آزادیاں کہاں وہ اب اپنے گھونسلے کی
اپنی خوشی سے آنا، اپنی خوشی سے جانا

— Translation —
Gone are the freedoms of our own nests
Where we could come and go at our own pleasure
meaning: "Iqbal Verse #12" by Allama Iqbal is a inspirational urdu poem consisting of 6 lines. This Urdu verse by Allama Iqbal showcases the rich tradition of South Asian poetry. The poet masterfully employs Urdu literary conventions to convey profound emotional depth. Beginning with "آزادیاں کہاں وہ اب اپنے گھونسلے کی اپنی خوشی سے آنا، اپنی خوشی سے جانا...", this piece explores themes of inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional resonance. The work invites contemplation on the deeper currents of life, love, and the human condition. Allama Iqbal's celebrated body of poetry continues to inspire readers across generations and cultures, and this particular work stands as a powerful example of their artistic vision.
